name: Ruy Lopez
description: One of the oldest and most respected openings in chess. White develops the bishop to b5, pressuring the knight that defends e5. A lifetime of rich, strategic play flows from these first moves.

moves.instruction: Control the center with the king's pawn. Opens lines for the bishop and queen.
Black mirrors, claiming an equal share of the center and freeing the kingside pieces.
Develop the knight toward the center and attack e5 — White's first real threat.
Defend e5 with the knight, matching White's development tempo for tempo.
The Ruy Lopez. The bishop pins the defender of e5, putting long-term pressure on Black.
The Morphy Defense — question the bishop. Gain space and time on the queenside.
Retreat along the diagonal, keeping the tension on the c6-knight.
Develop and threaten e4, counterattacking instead of passively defending.
Castle for king safety. White tucks the king away and brings the rook toward the center.
Develop the bishop, preparing to castle and complete kingside development.
Defend e4 with the rook, clearing the back rank for further action.
Kick the bishop and gain queenside space — a key theme in the Lopez.
Bishop retreats but stays active, eyeing the f7 and d5 weak points.
Open the diagonal for the c8-bishop and solidify e5 before pushing back.
Prepare d4 to build a strong pawn center — the heart of White's plan.
Black castles. The main closed Ruy Lopez starting position has been reached.
